title: Technical Superintendent
description:
Roles & Responsibilities:

Responsible for the Technical supervision of the set of vessels allocated ,always guided by the Company policies and Code of Ethics, for safe operation of the vessels and zero harm to environment, people and assets.

  • Responsible for the Technical supervision of the set of vessels allocated ,always guided by the Company policies and Code of Ethics, for safe operation of the vessels and zero harm to environment, people and assets.
  • Take active part in the Safety, Quality and Environmental Culture within the Company and vessels and Ensuring that the vessels always comply with Flag and International Maritime Regulations.
  • Ensuring day to day activities - PMS, Defect reporting and close out, Critical spares, Risk assessment, Environment reporting, Voyage reporting, Ship certificates and Class Status, Lub oil and bunker analysis, are followed up closely; Ensuring prompt close out of defects noted in Class, Internal & External audits, PSC, Oil Major vetting.
  • Ensuring compliance with company KPIs and Ensuring Technical inspections as SMS requirement. Additional may be necessary example for dry docking, damage repairs, upgradation.
  • Ensuring Lub oils, stores, spares and servicing and repair requirements for the vessels are promptly and efficiently met, guided by the budget allocated for the various items.
  • Ensuring Class Surveys are arranged on time and convenient location. Dry dock list should be kept updated at all times.
  • As emergency team member for risk control on operation and maintenance activities, and may be backup for other colleagues as emergency arrangement


Requirements:

  • Minimum 3 Years Ship Management on tankers operation or Minimum 5 years ship management experiences as equivalent experiences or Minimum 3 years Management Level as COC 1 or Minimum 5 years Management level if COC 2
  • Minimum 3 Years Technical Superintendent experience for Tanker Vessels
  • Graduate and/or advanced diploma and/or above academic grade in Technical related subjects as equivalent qualification
  • Good Communication, Analytical and Team Player

postedCompany.description:
Mondoil Ship Management Pte. Ltd. is a Singapore-incorporated company . We specialize in comprehensive shipping services, including the chartering of ships and boats with crew, freight logistics, and ship brokering services.


As a dynamic and forward-looking organization, we currently manage three fleets and are actively expanding our fleet with a strategic goal of operating up to ten vessels in the near future. Our growth is driven by a commitment to operational excellence, safety, and sustainable maritime practices.

• Stable & Healthy Company: We are financially sound and steadily growing, offering long-term career stability.
• Positive Work Environment: Our team thrives in a collaborative, respectful, and inclusive culture.
• Career Growth: As we expand our fleet, we are creating new opportunities for advancement and leadership.
• Strategic Vision: Be part of a company that is shaping the future of regional shipping and logistics.


We are looking for passionate, skilled, and dedicated professionals to join our team and grow with us. Whether you're an experienced maritime manager or a motivated newcomer to the industry, Mondoil Ship Management offers a rewarding career path in a thriving sector
postedCompany.name: MONDOIL SHIP MANAGEMENT PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
